channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Ontario Man Charged in Canada Day Attack on Muslim Family
item.description: A man from Ontario has been charged following a Canada Day attack on a Muslim family, according to a report by CTV Northern Ontario. The incident occurred during a public celebration, where the suspec...

item.title: Pakistan warns Iran against Houthi attacks on Saudi Arabia
item.description: Pakistan has conveyed to Iran that Houthi attacks on Saudi Arabia are considered strikes on Pakistan itself, according to a Pakistani official. The country's top civil and military leaders have emphas...

item.title: GSIS Expands Digital Services to Improve Public Administration
item.description: The article discusses the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) and its evolving role in modernizing government services. It highlights GSIS's initiatives to digitize public administration, strea...

item.title: Sydney Sweeney and Scooter Braun's Feud with Taylor Swift: Underwear as a Symbol of the Conflict
item.description: The article details the ongoing feud between Taylor Swift and Scooter Braun, her former record executive boyfriend, following his 2019 acquisition of her master recordings. Sydney Sweeney, Braun's gir...

item.title: Students and Bidar Parisara Vahini Launch School Environmental Campaign
item.description: A plantation drive was organised in Hallikhed (B) of Humnabad taluk in Bidar district on July 16, 2026, as part of an environmental awareness programme initiated by Bidar Parisara Vahini. The event, h...

item.title: Banyana Banyana Focuses on Squad Management and Youth Integration Ahead of WAFCON
item.description: Coach Desiree Ellis has emphasized the importance of squad management and integrating young talent for Banyana Banyana's upcoming WAFCON campaign in Morocco. The team, which will compete for a spot in...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk's hospital transfer after 20 days of treatment
item.description: The article reports that Sonam Wangchuk, a prominent figure, was transferred to Safdarjung hospital after 20 days of treatment. The transfer was necessitated by his deteriorating health condition, whi...

item.title: Earthquake strikes southern Mexico near Guatemala, causing minor injuries but no major damage
item.description: A powerful 7.3 magnitude earthquake struck Mexico's southern Pacific coast on Friday, near the Guatemalan border, sending tremors across a vast region from Mexico City to El Salvador. While authoritie...

item.title: Legal scholar's argument on birthright citizenship sparks debate
item.description: A law professor has criticized a legal theory proposed in a Trump-aligned amicus brief filed in a Supreme Court case regarding birthright citizenship. The argument, advanced by legal scholar Richard E...

item.title: Daily Horoscopes for July 18, 2026: Insights for Each Zodiac Sign
item.description: The article provides daily horoscope predictions for various zodiac signs on July 18, 2026. ARIES is advised to grant family members independence, recognizing it as an emotional investment. TAURUS is ...

item.title: Entrepreneur Shares Experience of Supporting Family After Husband Lost Pilot Job
item.description: Bunmi George, a fitness entrepreneur, opened up about her husband's decision to join her company, Shredder Gang, after losing his job as a pilot. The couple faced financial challenges, with George red...

item.title: Former federal NDP candidate Trisha Estabrooks announces provincial run
item.description: Trisha Estabrooks, a former federal NDP candidate and Edmonton Public School Board chair, has announced her intention to seek the Alberta NDP nomination for Edmonton-Strathcona in the 2027 provincial ...

item.title: Iran Escalates Attacks on Gulf States Amid US-Iran Tensions

item.title: Military Rescues 9 Abducted Victims in Katsina Forest Operation
item.description: Troops from the Joint Task Force North West, Operation Fansan Yamma, successfully rescued nine victims who escaped from their abductors during intelligence-led operations in Katsina State. The victims...

item.title: Swiss rider Mauro Schmid clinches Tour de France stage 13 victory as Tom Pidcock surges in overall standings
item.description: In a thrilling conclusion to the 13th stage of the 113th Tour de France, Swiss rider Mauro Schmid of Team Jayco AlUla secured victory in a two-man sprint finish against Colombian Harold Tejada. The st...

item.title: Indigenous group seeks cultural land restitution in B.C. negotiations
item.description: The Osoyoos Indian Band is engaged in land negotiations with the British Columbia government to reclaim culturally significant sites and ancestral burial grounds. Chief Clarence Louie emphasized that ...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk shifted to hospital as hunger strike enters day 21; Delhi Police detain Abhijeet Dipke amid Jantar Mantar protest
item.description: Climate activist and educator Sonam Wangchuk was shifted to Safdarjung Hospital by the Delhi Police after his health reportedly deteriorated during his indefinite hunger strike, which entered its 21st...

item.title: Poland's Visa-Free List Includes Three African Nations
item.description: Poland has released an official list of countries whose citizens can enter the country without a visa. Only three African nations—Mauritius, Seychelles, and São Tomé and Príncipe—are included. These c...

item.title: Mandela Day Family Outings: Exploring South Africa's Historical Sites
item.description: This article highlights six significant historical sites across South Africa where families can engage in meaningful educational experiences during Mandela Day. The focus is on sites that shaped Nelso...

item.title: Mkapa’s Legacy Impacts National Health Initiatives
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing influence of former Tanzanian President Benjamin Mkapa's policies on the country's healthcare sector. Nchimbi, a key figure in health advocacy, highlights how Mkapa's...
